title: Add --compact flag to claude trail for condensed 3-line output
essence: Running `claude trail <id> --compact` now emits a terse 3-line summary (title / Verdict / Cost) instead of the full multi-section markdown trail. The flag is mutually exclusive with --format json, --out, and --since, with clear error messages for each conflict. A golden-file regression test locks the output format permanently.

checkpoints.label: main
checkpoints.caption: claude trail --compact emits exactly 3 lines; conflicting flags exit non-zero with clear stderr
checkpoints.beforeNote: Before this change, `claude trail <id>` always produced a full multi-section markdown document (## Summary, ## Phases, ## Cost rollup, ## Git activity, etc.). There was no way to get a quick, machine-parseable verdict+cost summary. Running with any combination of flags (--format json, --out, --since) silently accepted all of them.
checkpoints.afterNote:
After this change:

1. `claude trail INIT-FIXTURE-1 --compact` prints exactly:
   ```
   # Trail — INIT-FIXTURE-1
   Verdict: approve
   Cost: $0.24
   ```
   Exit 0. Output is byte-for-byte locked to `tests/fixtures/INIT-FIXTURE-1.trail-compact.golden.md`.

2. When no cycle.end event exists, compact gracefully falls back to `Verdict: (unknown)` / `Cost: $0.00`.

3. `--compact` is mutually exclusive with `--format json`, `--out`, and `--since`. Each combination exits non-zero with a clear error naming both conflicting flags.

4. Non-compact (`claude trail INIT-FIXTURE-1` without the flag) is unchanged — the existing golden file still matches byte-for-byte (AC12 regression guard).

To convince yourself: `grep -r 'renderCompact\|compactFlag\|--compact' src/` shows the implementation; `npm test` (245/245 pass) proves all 12 ACs green.

acceptanceCriteria: AC1 (WI-1): --compact with approve verdict emits exact 3-line output, exit 0
AC2 (WI-1): --compact with no cycle.end emits Verdict: (unknown) / Cost: $0.00, exit 0
AC3 (WI-2): --compact + --format json exits non-zero, stderr names both flags
AC4 (WI-2): --compact + --out exits non-zero, stderr names both flags
AC5 (WI-2): --compact + --since exits non-zero, stderr names both flags
AC6 (WI-2): --compact alone exits 0 with 3-line output (regression guard)
AC8 (WI-3): golden file contains exactly 3 non-empty lines
AC9 (WI-3): --compact + --format json exits non-zero from fixture dir
AC10 (WI-3): --compact + --out exits non-zero from fixture dir
AC11 (WI-3): --compact + --since exits non-zero from fixture dir
AC12 (WI-3): without --compact, full trail still matches existing golden file
